states.name: Alabama
states.licensing_note: GC license required for projects over $50K (residential) or $50K commercial; statewide HVAC, plumbing, electrical licensing administered by Alabama Board.

states.name: Alaska
states.licensing_note: All contractors performing work over $10K must be registered with the state; specialty licenses required for plumbing, electrical, mechanical.

states.name: Arizona
states.licensing_note: Arizona Registrar of Contractors requires license for all work over $1,000; separate residential and commercial licenses for HVAC, plumbing, electrical.

states.name: Arkansas
states.licensing_note: GC license required for projects over $50K; specialty trades (HVAC, plumbing, electrical) licensed separately.

states.name: California
states.licensing_note: Contractors State License Board (CSLB) requires license for any work over $500. Separate C-class licenses for HVAC (C-20), plumbing (C-36), electrical (C-10), roofing (C-39), and 40+ other trades. Strictest licensing in the US.

states.name: Colorado
states.licensing_note: No statewide GC license; cities and counties handle licensing. State licenses HVAC (mechanical), plumbing, electrical separately.

states.name: Connecticut
states.licensing_note: Statewide GC registration required; HVAC, plumbing, electrical licensed by Department of Consumer Protection.

states.name: Delaware
states.licensing_note: Business license required for all contractors; statewide HVAC, plumbing, electrical licensing.

states.name: Florida
states.licensing_note: Department of Business and Professional Regulation (DBPR) licenses GCs, building contractors, HVAC (Class A/B), plumbing, electrical, roofing. Hurricane-zone roofing has additional certification requirements.

states.name: Georgia
states.licensing_note: Statewide licensing for GC, HVAC, plumbing, electrical, low-voltage. Georgia State Construction Industry Licensing Board oversees most trades.

states.name: Hawaii
states.licensing_note: Contractors License Board requires statewide license for all trades over $1,000. Specialty licenses for HVAC (C-52), plumbing (C-37), electrical (C-13), roofing (C-42).

states.name: Idaho
states.licensing_note: Statewide GC registration required for jobs over $2,000; HVAC, plumbing, electrical licensed by Division of Occupational and Professional Licenses.

states.name: Illinois
states.licensing_note: No statewide GC license; Chicago and other municipalities have local licensing. State licenses plumbing, roofing. Electrical licensing handled by city/county.

states.name: Indiana
states.licensing_note: No statewide GC license; Indianapolis and major cities license locally. State licenses plumbing only at the statewide level.

states.name: Iowa
states.licensing_note: Contractor registration required for any work over $2,000. HVAC, plumbing, electrical licensed by separate boards.

states.name: Kansas
states.licensing_note: No statewide GC license. Cities (Wichita, Kansas City) handle most licensing. Statewide HVAC, plumbing, electrical license.

states.name: Kentucky
states.licensing_note: Statewide HVAC, plumbing, electrical licensing; GC licensing handled at city/county level.

states.name: Louisiana
states.licensing_note: Louisiana State Licensing Board for Contractors licenses GC, mechanical (HVAC), plumbing, electrical, roofing for projects over $50K. Hurricane-zone roofing has additional requirements.

states.name: Maine
states.licensing_note: No statewide GC license. State licenses HVAC (mechanical), plumbing, electrical separately.

states.name: Maryland
states.licensing_note: Home Improvement Commission license required for residential work over $500. Statewide HVAC, plumbing, electrical licensing.

states.name: Massachusetts
states.licensing_note: Home Improvement Contractor (HIC) registration required for residential work over $1,000. State licenses HVAC, plumbing, electrical, sheet metal separately.

states.name: Michigan
states.licensing_note: Residential builder license required for residential work over $600. Statewide HVAC (mechanical), plumbing, electrical, roofing licensing.

states.name: Minnesota
states.licensing_note: Residential building contractor license required. Department of Labor and Industry licenses HVAC, plumbing, electrical, residential roofing.

states.name: Mississippi
states.licensing_note: GC license required for projects over $50K. Mississippi State Board of Contractors licenses major trades.

states.name: Missouri
states.licensing_note: No statewide GC license. Major cities (Kansas City, St. Louis) license locally. Statewide HVAC, plumbing, electrical licensing.

states.name: Montana
states.licensing_note: Contractor registration required. State licenses HVAC, plumbing, electrical separately.

states.name: Nebraska
states.licensing_note: Contractor registration required. Cities handle most GC licensing. Statewide HVAC, plumbing, electrical.

states.name: Nevada
states.licensing_note: State Contractors Board licenses all trades. C-1 through C-21 classifications for HVAC, plumbing, electrical, roofing, GC.

states.name: New Hampshire
states.licensing_note: No statewide GC license. State licenses electrical, plumbing only. HVAC and roofing rely on city/county.

states.name: New Jersey
states.licensing_note: Home Improvement Contractor (HIC) registration required for residential work. State licenses HVAC, plumbing, electrical separately.

states.name: New Mexico
states.licensing_note: Construction Industries Division licenses all trades. Separate licenses for GC (GB-98, GB-2), HVAC (MM-98), plumbing (MM-3), electrical (EE-98).

states.name: New York
states.licensing_note: No statewide GC license; NYC and other cities license locally. State licenses asbestos, lead, mold. HVAC, plumbing, electrical licensed at city/county level — NYC has its own DOB licensing.

states.name: North Carolina
states.licensing_note: GC license required for projects over $30K. NC Licensing Board for General Contractors. Separate boards for HVAC, plumbing, electrical.

states.name: North Dakota
states.licensing_note: Contractor license required for jobs over $4,000. Statewide HVAC, plumbing, electrical licensing.

states.name: Ohio
states.licensing_note: No statewide GC license. State licenses HVAC, plumbing, electrical, hydronics, refrigeration through Ohio Construction Industry Licensing Board.

states.name: Oklahoma
states.licensing_note: Construction Industries Board licenses HVAC, plumbing, electrical, roofing. No statewide GC license.

states.name: Oregon
states.licensing_note: Construction Contractors Board (CCB) requires license for any construction work. Specialty endorsements for HVAC, plumbing, electrical.

states.name: Pennsylvania
states.licensing_note: Home Improvement Contractor (HIC) registration required for residential work over $5,000. State licenses electrical only at statewide level; HVAC and plumbing licensed locally.

states.name: Rhode Island
states.licensing_note: Contractor Registration and Licensing Board licenses all trades. Separate boards for HVAC, plumbing, electrical.

states.name: South Carolina
states.licensing_note: GC license required for residential projects over $200 or commercial over $5,000. SC Department of Labor licenses HVAC, plumbing, electrical.

states.name: South Dakota
states.licensing_note: No statewide GC license. State licenses plumbing, electrical. HVAC handled locally.

states.name: Tennessee
states.licensing_note: GC license required for projects over $25K. TN Board for Licensing Contractors licenses HVAC, plumbing, electrical, roofing separately.

states.name: Texas
states.licensing_note: No statewide GC license; cities and counties license locally. Statewide HVAC, plumbing, electrical licensing through TDLR. Roofing has voluntary RCAT certification.

states.name: Utah
states.licensing_note: Division of Occupational and Professional Licensing requires statewide license for all contractors. B100 GC, S350 HVAC, S210 plumbing, S200 electrical, S330 roofing.

states.name: Vermont
states.licensing_note: No statewide GC license. State licenses plumbing, electrical.

states.name: Virginia
states.licensing_note: DPOR licenses Class A/B/C contractors (different project thresholds). HVAC, plumbing, electrical, GC all require statewide license.

states.name: Washington
states.licensing_note: Department of Labor & Industries licenses all contractors. Specialty certifications for HVAC, plumbing, electrical, roofing.

states.name: West Virginia
states.licensing_note: Contractor Licensing Board licenses all contractors for projects over $2,500. Separate trade licenses for HVAC, plumbing, electrical.

states.name: Wisconsin
states.licensing_note: Dwelling Contractor Certification required for residential work. Department of Safety and Professional Services licenses HVAC, plumbing, electrical.

states.name: Wyoming
states.licensing_note: No statewide GC license. State licenses electrical only at statewide level. HVAC and plumbing licensed by cities.
